content:
The user wants me to extract the table of contents from the provided image and format it as JSON.

1.  **Identify the image content**: The image is a table of contents page from a technical datasheet, specifically for STM32F411xC and STM32F411xE microcontrollers.

2.  **Locate the main title**: The main title "Contents" is at the top.

3.  **Locate the table**: The main body of the page is a table listing sections and subsections with their corresponding page numbers.

4.  **Extract the data**: I need to go through each row of the table and extract the section number, title, and page number.

    *   **1 Introduction**: 11
    *   **2 Description**: 12
        *   **2.1 Compatibility with STM32F4 series**: 14
    *   **3 Functional overview**: 17
        *   **3.1 Arm® Cortex®-M4 with FPU core with embedded flash memory and SRAM**: 17
        *   **3.2 Adaptive real-time memory accelerator (ART Accelerator)**: 17
        *   **3.3 Batch acquisition mode (BAM)**: 17
        *   **3.4 Memory protection unit**: 18
        *   **3.5 Embedded flash memory**: 18
        *   **3.6 CRC (cyclic redundancy check) calculation unit**: 18
        *   **3.7 Embedded SRAM**: 18
        *   **3.8 Multi-AHB bus matrix**: 19
        *   **3.9 DMA controller (DMA)**: 19
        *   **3.10 Nested vectored interrupt controller (NVIC)**: 20
        *   **3.11 External interrupt/event controller (EXTI)**: 20
        *   **3.12 Clocks and startup**: 20
        *   **3.13 Boot modes**: 21
        *   **3.14 Power supply schemes**: 21
        *   **3.15 Power supply supervisor**: 22
            *   **3.15.1 Internal reset ON**: 22
            *   **3.15.2 Internal reset OFF**: 22
        *   **3.16 Voltage regulator**: 23
            *   **3.16.1 Regulator ON**: 23
            *   **3.16.2 Regulator OFF**: 23
            *   **3.16.3 Regulator ON/OFF and internal power supply supervisor availability**: 26
        *   **3.17 Real-time clock (RTC) and backup registers**: 26
        *   **3.18 Low-power modes**: 27
        *   **3.19 VBAT operation**: 27
        *   **3.20 Timers and watchdogs**: 28

5.  **Format the output**: Create a JSON object with keys "title", "source", and "text". The "text" will contain the extracted table of contents.

6.  **Verify**: Check for any missed entries or formatting errors. The extracted data looks complete and accurate based on the image. The footer information (ST logo, DS10314 Rev 8, 3/151) is not part of the table of contents but good to note for context. I will focus on the main content.

7.  **Final JSON construction**:
    ```json
    {"title": "Contents", "source": "None", "text": "Contents\n\n1 Introduction …… 11\n\n2 Description …… 12\n2.1 Compatibility with STM32F4 series …… 14\n\n3 Functional overview …… 17\n3.1 Arm® Cortex®-M4 with FPU core with embedded flash memory and SRAM …… 17\n3.2 Adaptive real-time memory accelerator (ART Accelerator) …… 17\n3.3 Batch acquisition mode (BAM) …… 17\n3.4 Memory protection unit …… 18\n3.5 Embedded flash memory …… 18\n3.6 CRC (cyclic redundancy check) calculation unit …… 18\n3.7 Embedded SRAM …… 18\n3.8 Multi-AHB bus matrix …… 19\n3.9 DMA controller (DMA) …… 19\n3.10 Nested vectored interrupt controller (NVIC) …… 20\n3.11 External interrupt/event controller (EXTI) …… 20\n3.12 Clocks and startup …… 20\n3.13 Boot modes …… 21\n3.14 Power supply schemes …… 21\n3.15 Power supply supervisor …… 22\n3.15.1 Internal reset ON …… 22\n3.15.2 Internal reset OFF …… 22\n3.16 Voltage regulator …… 23\n3.16.1 Regulator ON …… 23\n3.16.2 Regulator OFF …… 23\n3.16.3 Regulator ON/OFF and internal power supply supervisor availability …… 26\n3.17 Real-time clock (RTC) and backup registers …… 26\n3.18 Low-power modes …… 27\n3.19 VBAT operation …… 27\n3.20 Timers and watchdogs …… 28"}
    ```
    Wait, I should format the text to be more readable, perhaps using newlines for each entry.

    Let's refine the text content to be a clean list.
    Contents
    1 Introduction 11
    2 Description 12
    2.1 Compatibility with STM32F4 series 14
    3 Functional overview 17
    3.1 Arm® Cortex®-M4 with FPU core with embedded flash memory and SRAM 17
    3.2 Adaptive real-time memory accelerator (ART Accelerator) 17
    3.3 Batch acquisition mode (BAM) 17
    3.4 Memory protection unit 18
    3.5 Embedded flash memory 18
    3.6 CRC (cyclic redundancy check) calculation unit 18
    3.7 Embedded SRAM 18
    3.8 Multi-AHB bus matrix 19
    3.9 DMA controller (DMA) 19
    3.10 Nested vectored interrupt controller (NVIC) 20
    3.11 External interrupt/event controller (EXTI) 20
    3.12 Clocks and startup 20
    3.13 Boot modes 21
    3.14 Power supply schemes 21
    3.15 Power supply supervisor 22
    3.15.1 Internal reset ON 22
    3.15.2 Internal reset OFF 22
    3.16 Voltage regulator 23
    3.16.1 Regulator ON 23
    3.16.2 Regulator OFF 23
    3.16.3 Regulator ON/OFF and internal power supply supervisor availability 26
    3.17 Real-time clock (RTC) and backup registers 26
    3.18 Low-power modes 27
    3.19 VBAT operation 27
    3.20 Timers and watchdogs 28

    This looks good. I will output the JSON now.
```json
[{"title": "Contents", "source": "None", "text": "Contents\n\n1 Introduction …… 11\n\n2 Description …… 12\n2.1 Compatibility with STM32F4 series …… 14\n\n3 Functional overview …… 17\n3.1 Arm® Cortex®-M4 with FPU core with embedded flash memory and SRAM …… 17\n3.2 Adaptive real-time memory accelerator (ART Accelerator) …… 17\n3.3 Batch acquisition mode (BAM) …… 17\n3.4 Memory protection unit …… 18\n3.5 Embedded flash memory …… 18\n3.6 CRC (cyclic redundancy check) calculation unit …… 18\n3.7 Embedded SRAM …… 18\n3.8 Multi-AHB bus matrix …… 19\n3.9 DMA controller (DMA) …… 19\n3.10 Nested vectored interrupt controller (NVIC) …… 20\n3.11 External interrupt/event controller (EXTI) …… 20\n3.12 Clocks and startup …… 20\n3.13 Boot modes …… 21\n3.14 Power supply schemes …… 21\n3.15 Power supply supervisor …… 22\n3.15.1 Internal reset ON …… 22\n3.15.2 Internal reset OFF …… 22\n3.16 Voltage regulator …… 23\n3.16.1 Regulator ON …… 23\n3.16.2 Regulator OFF …… 23\n3.16.3 Regulator ON/OFF and internal power supply supervisor availability …… 26\n3.17 Real-time clock (RTC) and backup registers …… 26\n3.18 Low-power modes …… 27\n3.19 VBAT operation …… 27\n3.20 Timers and watchdogs …… 28"}]
```
